name: Steel Bank Common Lisp (SBCL)
summary: Long-lived production aggressive optimiser in the Lisp family — descended from CMUCL's Python compiler. Block compiler with whole-function-level type inference, type-directed open coding, and direct native code generation. Hybrid REPL-style interactivity and aggressive AOT optimisation. ITA Software's QPX (Google Flights) is the canonical large deployment.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: **Steel Bank Common Lisp** (SBCL, derived from CMUCL since 2000) is one of the longest-lived production aggressive optimisers in any language family — a Common Lisp implementation with whole-function-level type inference, automatic specialisation based on inferred types, and direct native code generation without an intermediate IR family in the LLVM/Cranelift sense. The compiler is itself written in Common Lisp and is the descendant of CMUCL's *Python* compiler (no relation to the language Python).

sections.title: Block compiler architecture
sections.content:
SBCL compiles top-level forms incrementally as they are read, accumulating a type environment across the program. A `(defun foo ...)` form compiles immediately to native code with the types known at that point; later definitions extend the environment and may trigger recompilation hints. This makes SBCL a hybrid of **REPL-style interactivity and aggressive AOT optimisation** — every interactive session is silently type-inferring across thousands of definitions.

**Type-directed open coding**: when the inferred type of a Lisp value is sufficiently constrained (a known integer range, a fixed-shape array, a specific class), SBCL replaces generic dispatch with direct machine instructions. `(+ x y)` where both operands are inferred fixnum becomes a single `addq` on x86-64; `(aref vec i)` where `vec` is a typed `(simple-array (unsigned-byte 8))` becomes a direct array load with type-elided bounds checking.

sections.title: No separate JIT tier
sections.content:
SBCL is fully AOT — all code is compiled before execution — but the compilation is incremental and integrated into the REPL. The lesson is that "compile aggressively at function definition time" can substitute for tiered JIT for languages with sufficient type-inference discipline at the function boundary.

**Fully self-hosted**: the SBCL compiler is written in Common Lisp; bootstrapping reproducibility is checked via cross-comparison across host implementations (SBCL, CCL, ECL, CLISP). The bootstrap chain is closer to Forth metacompilation than to mrustc-style alternative-compiler bootstrap.

sections.title: Production deployments
sections.content:
Production deployment includes ITA Software's QPX search engine (acquired by Google for Google Flights), Genworks GDL (CAD), Mirai (animation), several quantitative trading firms, and various scientific-computing pipelines. Performance is competitive with C on numeric kernels with proper type declarations.

The CMUCL→SBCL→CCL lineage is one of the most mature production-FP-compiler stories outside the ML family, and a useful counterweight to the JIT-everywhere assumption common in modern dynamic-language compiler design.
